台股與集中市場 ·

地緣與籌碼震盪免驚！法人：科技龍頭AI變現蓄勢待發 長多格局未變 | 鉅亨網 - 台股新聞Analysts: Big tech poised to monetize AI; bulls say turbulence won’t overturn the longer-term bullish case

https://news.cnyes.com/news/id/6461179

市場進入高檔震盪與結構分化輪動，面對中東地緣政治帶來的通膨疑慮與台股急漲後籌碼沉澱，安聯投信台股團隊認為，短線震盪不改長線多頭。受惠四大 CSP 業者上調資本支出、AI 應用拉動算力需求，下半年硬體供應鏈獲利仍有上修空間；建議將短線利空視為逢低布局時機，鎖定基本面扎實的 AI 關鍵零組件龍頭、高息成長股與主動式 ETF，動態調整配置以極大化風險調整後報酬。

安聯台灣大壩基金經理人蕭惠中指出，美伊對和談條件分歧仍大，協議遲遲難簽，霍爾木茲海峽仍封鎖；若僵局拖長，即便中東貿易恢復正常，先前停產的油田設施重啟與運輸回穩也需時間。此不必然迫使升息壓通膨，但將使美國聯準會利率態度更趨謹慎。

蕭惠中亦表示，台股 4 月漲勢驚人，月下旬起個股震盪加劇，短期將進入整理以利籌碼沉澱；能否辨識具實質基本面成長的產業與個股，將攸關投資組合能否穩定獲利，選股仍是核心，可在指數震盪時承接遭錯殺的穩健成長標的。

回到基本面，蕭惠中說，四大 CSP 業者於四月底同步公布財報，其中 AWS 與 Google 雲服務營收年增亮眼；四家公司皆重申 AI 需求強勁、算力供不應求，且在記憶體等原料成本高檔下，今年資本支出小幅上調。

展望後市，蕭惠中認為，相關業者資本支出可望再明顯增加，反映企業導入 AI Agent 趨勢成形，算力消耗呈指數級成長，並成為 CSP 高額 AI 投資首批具變現力的應用，因此長線仍看好台股在全球 AI 晶片與配套硬體供應鏈的角色。

布局方面，蕭惠中表示，基本面持續上修是此波台股上漲主因，組合仍以關鍵零組件龍頭為主軸；短線雖可能因股價急漲或中東局勢未定而波動加大，但下半年進入 GPU 與 ASIC 晶片大量出貨期，供應鏈獲利仍有上修機會。

安聯台灣主動式 ETF（00993A-TW）經理人施政廷表示，地緣風險市場反應漸趨鈍化，資金正重回企業獲利與 AI 題材；只要科技龍頭訂單能見度維持明朗，短線利空多半是可逢低布局的窗口，而非多頭反轉訊號。

策略上，施政廷指出，在個股高度分化環境中，量化因子搭配主動選股將更凸顯主動管理優勢；透過多因子模型，將組合因子權重與 Tracking Error 聯合最佳化、動態調整持股，為現階段因應資金輪動與極端波動、並最大化 Risk-adjusted Return 的有效做法。

The market is in elevated volatility with structural rotation and divergence. Despite inflation worries tied to Middle East geopolitics and post-rally consolidation in Taiwan equities, Allianz Global Investors Taiwan’s Taiwan equity team argues short-term swings do not derail the longer-term bull case. With four major CSPs lifting capex and AI applications pulling compute demand, hardware supply-chain profits still have room for upward revision in the second half; treat near-term negatives as chances to build positions on dips, focusing on AI critical-component leaders with solid fundamentals, dividend growers, and active ETFs, and rebalance dynamically to maximize risk-adjusted returns.

Hsiao Hui-chung, portfolio manager of the Allianz Taiwan Dam Fund, said the U.S. and Iran remain far apart on negotiation terms, making a deal elusive and leaving the Strait of Hormuz blocked; if the impasse persists, even if Middle East trade normalizes, restarting previously idled oil facilities and stabilizing shipping will take time. That does not necessarily force rate hikes to fight inflation, but it will make the Federal Reserve’s posture on interest rates more cautious.

Hsiao added that Taiwan stocks surged remarkably in April, with individual-stock swings intensifying late in the month; in the near term the market is likely to consolidate so positioning can settle. Identifying industries and names with genuine fundamental growth will be key to steady portfolio returns—stock selection remains central—and index volatility can offer entries into robust growers that have been unfairly sold off.

On fundamentals, Hsiao said the four major CSPs reported together around end-April; AWS and Google Cloud posted standout year-on-year revenue growth. All four reiterated strong AI demand and tight compute supply, and given elevated memory and other input costs, they modestly raised capex guidance for this year.

Looking ahead, Hsiao expects related providers’ capex could rise further materially, reflecting AI Agent adoption taking shape, exponential growth in compute consumption, and among CSPs’ heavy AI spending one of the first applications with clearer monetization—so he remains constructive long term on Taiwan’s role in global AI chips and supporting hardware supply chains.

On positioning, Hsiao said upward earnings revisions have been the main driver of this Taiwan rally, with portfolios still anchored in critical-component leaders. Near-term volatility may increase amid sharp price gains or Middle East uncertainty, but the second half enters a heavy shipment window for GPU and ASIC chips, leaving further upside for supply-chain earnings revisions.

Shih Cheng-ting, portfolio manager of the Allianz Taiwan Active ETF (00993A-TW), said markets are growing numb to geopolitical risk and capital is rotating back to corporate earnings and AI themes. As long as order visibility for tech leaders stays clear, short-term negatives are mostly dip-buying windows rather than signals of a bull-market reversal.

Strategically, Shih said in a highly differentiated stock environment, quantitative factors paired with active selection better showcase active management’s edge; using multi-factor models to jointly optimize portfolio factor weights and Tracking Error and to adjust holdings dynamically is an effective way to navigate capital rotation and extreme volatility while maximizing Risk-adjusted Return.
